chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 2900 of 2025 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection 2. For hearing of CMA No.12891/2025 3. For hearing of main case 29.10.2025 Mr. Zia-ul-Haq Makhdoom, advocate for the petitioner Mr. Pervaiz Ahmed Memon, advocate for respondent Perusal of the prayer clause demonstrates that the relief sought is predicated on apprehensions and surmises and even the memorandum of petition fails to disclose an actionable clause. Upon being so confronted, learned counsel remained unable to displace the observation. While the learned counsel sought to argue a case for exports being restricted, however, failed to demonstrate any GDs having been filed before the respondents at Karachi; or in Sindh for that matter. There was a suggestion that GDs were filed at Chaman, Balochistan, but that the same had been withdrawn. Even though Chaman Balochistan does not fall within the territorial remit of this Court, counsel remained unable to assist as to what grievance remained if the petitioner had itself resiled. In summation, no case has been set forth before us to merit the invocation of the discretionary1 writ jurisdiction of this Court; therefore, this petition is hereby dismissed. Judge Judge Amjad 1Per Ijaz Ul Ahsan J. in Syed Iqbal Hussain Shah Gillani vs. PBC & Others reported as 2021 SCMR 425; Muhammad Fiaz Khan vs. Ajmer Khan & Another reported as 2010 SCMR 105.
